# Box Connector - Full Documentation

# Box Connector 5.3 Additional Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To use Box Connector in a Mule app, configure a global Box configuration element to use by as many
Box connectors as needed for your application.

## Configure the Box Connector Global Element

1.  Click the **Global Elements** tab at the base of the canvas.
2.  In the **Global Configuration Elements** screen, click **Create**.
3.  In the **Choose Global Type** wizard, expand Connector Configuration and select
    **Mule-box-connector Config** and click **OK**.  
    In the configuration screen for `OAUTH 2.0` with Authorization Code, grant type displays. This
    is the only configuration that Box Connector supports.
4.  Configure the properties as described here.

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>clientId</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the client identifier as assigned by the authorization server when the client application was registered.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>clientSecret</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the client applicationâs client secret.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The host where Box exposes its API.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>port</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port where Box services listen for incoming requests. Default: 443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>basePath</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The base path for the Box services' base URI.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>protocol</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The protocol which Box services follow. Default: HTTPS</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>localCallbackPath</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The local callback path. Default: /callback</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>localCallbackConfig</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The HTTP Listener configuration that is used to configure the local authorization URL.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>externalCallbackUrl</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The redirect URL that is configured in the client application.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>localAuthorizationUrl</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Local authorization URL that is used to initiate an <code>OAuth2</code> dance.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>authorizationUrl</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The authorization URL to request for an authorization code.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>accessTokenUrl</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the URL for requesting an access token.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    In the `OAUTH 2.0` configuration screen, the placeholder values refer to a configuration file
    `mule-artifact.properties` placed in the `src/main/resources` folder of your project.

    You can either enter your credentials into the global configuration properties, or reference a
    configuration file that contains these values.

    > [!NOTE] Use a configuration file for simpler maintenance and better reusability of your
    > project. Keeping these values in a separate file is useful if you need to deploy to different
    > environments where your access credentials are different, such as production, development, and
    > QA.

5.  Click **OK**.

## JWT Authentication

Authenticate your application directly to Box using a JSON Web Token (JWT).

The following steps describe how to configure Box Connector using JWT:

- [Create an App](#create-an-app)
- [Generate a Keypair](#generate-a-keypair)
- [Export and Add the Public Key](#export-and-add)

### Create an App

1.  Go to the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console).
2.  Select **Create New App**.
3.  Select **Custom App** for the type of application.
4.  Select **Server Authentication (with JWT)** for the type of authentication.
5.  Provide a name for the application.
6.  Click **Create App**.

![Custom app selection](_images/box-custom-app-select.png)

### Generate a Keypair

1.  Download [KeyStore Explorer](https://keystore-explorer.org).
2.  Open the application and select **Create a new KeyStore**.
3.  Select **JKS**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  Click **Save**.

    ![Create new JKS KeyStore selection](_images/keystore-jks.png)

6.  Click on **Tools** and select **Generate Key Pair**.
7.  Select **RSA** with a key size of 2048.

    ![RSA with a key size of 2048 selection](_images/rsa-2048.png)

8.  Click **OK** to prompt the **Generate Key Pair Certificate** dialog box to appear.
9.  Accept the default settings.

    ![Generate Key Pair Certificate dialog box](_images/generate-keypair-certificate.png)

10. Click on the **Name** icon and add the following information:
    - **Common Name (CN)**: mulesoft
    - **Organization Unit (OU)**: mulesoft
    - **Organization Name (O)**: mulesoft
    - **Locality Name (L)**: mulesoft
    - **State Name (ST)**: mulesoft
    - **Country (C)**: CA

11. Click **OK**.

    ![Information added to the Name icon dialog box](_images/name.png)

12. Click **Save** to prompt the **New Key Pair Entry Alias** dialog box to appear.
13. Enter **mulesoft** as the alias.

    ![Added mulesoft alias to New Key Pair Entry Alias dialog box](_images/keypair-alias.png)

14. Click **OK** to prompt the **New Key Pair Entry Password** dialog box to appear.
15. Enter a new password.

    ![Added password to New Key Pair Entry Password dialog box](_images/keypair-password.png)

16. Click **OK** to successfully generate your keypair.

### Export and Add the Public Key

1.  Right-click on the generated keypair and navigate to **Export** > **Export Public Key**.

    ![Navigation for Export Public Key](_images/export-public-key.png)

2.  Save your keystore and set a password.
3.  Open the exported public key in the editor and copy all the content.
4.  Go to the [Developer Console](https://app.box.com/developers/console).
5.  Go to the **Configuration** tab and scroll down to the **Add and Manage Public Keys** section.
6.  Click **Add a Public Key** and paste all the copied content.

    ![Add Public Key button](_images/add-public-key.png)

7.  Click **Verify and Save**.
8.  After successful verification, you will receive the **Public key id**.  
    You can also find other properties that depend on your application, such as **Entity id** and
    **Box sub type**, in the Developer Console on the **General Settings** tab in the **App Info**
    section and add them to the Studio configuration.

![Studio configuration](_images/studio-config.png)

## Next

Now that you have completed configuration, you can try out the Box Connector
[Examples](box-connector-examples).

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Box Connector 5.3 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Try out the following examples using Studio or XML.

## Anypoint Studio Example: Folders

This example shows how to create a folder, retrieve a folder, and delete a folder.

1.  Create a new Mule project in Anypoint Studio.
2.  Add the following properties to the `mule-artifact.properties` file and place it in the
    projectâs `src/main/resources` directory.

    ```text
    config.clientId=<Client ID>
    config.clientSecret=<Client Secret>
    ```

3.  Configure the HTTP Listener by adding a new HTTP global element. Click the **+** next to the
    Connector configuration field.
4.  Configure the global element according to the following table:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP_Listener_config</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>localhost</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>8081</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  Drag an HTTP Listener onto the canvas and configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP Listener</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the newly created HTTP Listener Configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><p><code>/boxdemo</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Set the folder properties for the folder you are going to create using a DataWeave component.
    Drag the **DataWeave** component next to the HTTP Listener and use the following script.

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	parent: {
    		id: 0
    	},
    	"type": "folder",
    	name: "SampleFolder"
    }
    ```

7.  Drag the **Create folder** operation of the Box Connector next to the DataWeave component to
    create a folder.
8.  Configure Box Connector by adding a new Box global element. Click the **+** next to the
    Connector configuration field.
9.  Configure the global element according to the following table.

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th><th>Comments</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>clientId</p></td><td><p><code>${config.clientId}</code></p></td><td><p>The client identifier as assigned by the authorization server when the client application was registered.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>clientSecret</p></td><td><p><code>${config.clientSecret}</code></p></td><td><p>The client applicationâs client secret.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>host</p></td><td><p><code>api.box.com</code></p></td><td><p>The default value. Leave as is.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>port</p></td><td><p><code>443</code></p></td><td><p>The default value. Donât change.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>basePath</p></td><td><p><code>/2.0</code></p></td><td><p>The default value. Donât change.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>protocol</p></td><td><p><code>HTTPS</code></p></td><td><p>The default value. Donât change.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>localCallbackPath</p></td><td><p><code>/callback</code></p></td><td><p>The default value. Donât change.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>localCallbackConfig</p></td><td><p><code>HTTP_Listener_config</code></p></td><td><p>Select the HTTP Listener configuration created earlier.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>externalCallbackUrl</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/callback</code></p></td><td><p>Use the redirect URL that is configured in the client application.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>localAuthorizationUrl</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/authorize</code></p></td><td><p>Enter this URL after deploying the Mule application to initiate <code>OAUTH2</code> dance.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>authorizationUrl</p></td><td><p><code>https://account.box.com/api/oauth2/authorize</code></p></td><td><p>The authorization URL to request for an authorization code.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>accessTokenUrl</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.box.com/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td><p>The access token URL to request an access token.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    The corresponding XML configuration should be as follows:

    ```xml
    <mule-box-connector:config name="Mule_box_connector_Config"
     doc:name="Mule-box-connector Config"
     property_clientId="#{config.clientId}"
     property_clientSecret="#{config.clientSecret}"
     property_localCallbackConfig="HTTP_Listener_config"
     property_externalCallbackUrl="http://localhost:8081/callback" />
    ```

10. In the properties editor of Box Connector, configure the parameters required for the **Create
    folder** operation:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Create folder</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the global Box Connector element that you created.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create folder request data</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

11. Drag a **Logger** component to log the newly created folder ID and configure the following
    properties:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Specifies the logger to use to log the created folder ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Generic</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p>Specifies the folder created with ID : <code>#[payload.id]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

12. Drag the **Get folder** operation of Box Connector next to the **Logger** component. This
    retrieves the folder information and configures the following properties:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Get folder</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the global Box Connector element that you created.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload.id]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

13. Drag a **Logger** component to log the retrieved folder information and configure the following
    properties:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Specifies the logger that logs the retrieved folder information.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Generic</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

14. Drag the Box Connector **Delete folder** operation to delete the folder that was just created
    and configure the following properties:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Delete folder</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>Basic Settings</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the global Box Connector element that you created.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><p>General</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload.id]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

15. Drag the **DataWeave** component to set the payload to display the result of the flow to the
    user, and use the following script:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	result : "Folder was created, retrieved and deleted successfully."
    }
    ```

16. Save and run the project as a Mule app.
17. Use the URL `http://localhost:8081/authorize` from a browser that you set for
    `localAuthorizationUrl` in the global Box configuration element to initiate the `OAUTH2` dance.

    This displays the page asking the Box user to either grant or deny access to the client
    application (which reads and writes all of files and folders stored in Box).

18. Click **Grant access to Box** to grant the read and write permissions.
19. Open a web browser and access `http://localhost:8081/boxdemo`.

    You should get a JSON response with the following content:

    Result: `"Folder created, retrieved and deleted successfully."`

## XML Example: Demonstrate Create Folder, Retrieve Folder, and Delete Folder Operations

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:mule-box-connector="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
  http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector/current/mule-mule-box-connector.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
		doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="localhost"
			port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<mule-box-connector:config name="Mule_box_connector_Config"
		doc:name="Mule-box-connector Config"
		property_clientId="#{config.clientId}"
		property_clientSecret="#{config.clientSecret}"
		property_localCallbackConfig="HTTP_Listener_config"
		property_externalCallbackUrl="http://localhost:8081/callback" />

	<flow name="Create-Get-Delete-Folder-Flow">

		<http:listener doc:name="HTTP Listener"
			path="/boxdemo"
			config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
		/>

		<ee:transform doc:name="DataWeave to set folder properties">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	parent: {
		id: 0
	},
	"type": "folder",
	name: "SampleFolder"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<box-connector:create-folder
			doc:name="Create folder" config-ref="Mule_box_connector_Config" />

		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger to log the created Folder ID"
			message="Folder created with ID : #[payload.id]" />

		<box-connector:get-folder doc:name="Get folder"
			config-ref="Mule_box_connector_Config" folder-id="#[payload.id]" />

		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger to log the retrieved folder info"
			message="#[payload]" />

		<box-connector:delete-folder
			doc:name="Delete folder" config-ref="Mule_box_connector_Config"
			folder-id="#[payload.id]" />

		<ee:transform doc:name="DataWeave to show the result">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	result : "Folder got created, retrieved and deleted successfully"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Example: Upload a File

This example demonstrates how to upload files to use with other Box operations you have created.

1.  Create a new Mule project in Anypoint Studio.
2.  Create a folder called `input` in `src/main/resources directory`.
3.  Copy the file you want to upload to the `input directory`.
4.  Configure the HTTP Listener by adding a new HTTP global element.
5.  Click the **Global Elements** tab at the bottom of the canvas.
6.  In the **Global Configuration Elements** screen, click **Create**.
7.  In the **Choose Global Type** wizard, expand the Connector Configuration.
8.  Select **HTTP Listener** and click **OK**.
9.  In the **HTTP Listener** screen configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP_Listener_config</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>localhost</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><p>8081</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

10. Drag an HTTP Listener onto the canvas and configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP Listener</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the HTTP Listener Configuration that has been previously created.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><p><code>/boxUploadFile</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

11. Login to Box and generate the Box developer token, which is active for 60 minutes.
12. Set this Developer Token using a DataWeave component.
13. Drag the DataWeave component next to the HTTP Listener and configure as follows.

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    		//Set the generated developer token here
    var developerToken = "{developer Token}"
    ---
    "Bearer " ++ developerToken
    ```

14. Drag a **Set Variable** component onto the canvas and configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Save developer token</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>developerToken</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

15. Configure the File connector by adding a new **File Global Element**.
16. Click the **Global Elements** tab at the bottom of the canvas.
17. From the **Global Configuration Elements** screen, click **Create**.
18. From the **Choose Global Type** wizard, expand Connector Configuration.
19. Select **File Config** and click **OK**.
20. In the File Config screen configure the following parameter:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Working Directory</p></td><td><p><code>${mule.home}/apps/${app.name}/</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

21. Drag a **Read File** operation onto the canvas and configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Read File</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the File Configuration that has been previously created in global elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Path</p></td><td><p><code>input/yourFileName</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

22. Transform this payload to a `multipart/form-data` type of request.
23. Specify a folder to upload by setting the ID of the parent folder.
24. Drag a DataWeave component next to the **Read File** component and use the following script:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output multipart/form-data
    ---
    {
      parts : {
      	attributes : {
      			headers : {
            		"Content-Type": "application/json"},
          		content : {
          			"name": message.attributes.fileName ,
    	// Set the ID of the parent folder. Use "0" for the root folder.
          	 		"parent": {
          	 		  "id":"0"
    				}
    			}
    	},
        file : {
          headers : {
            "Content-Disposition" : {
                "name": "file",
                "filename": message.attributes.fileName
            }
          },
          content : payload
        }
      }
    }
    ```

25. Configure the HTTP Request by adding a new HTTP Request global element.
26. Click the **Global Elements** tab at the bottom of the canvas.
27. In the **Global Configuration Elements** screen, click **Create**.
28. In the **Choose Global Type** wizard expand Connector Configuration, select **HTTP Request
    Configuration** and click **OK**.
29. In the HTTP Request Configuration screen configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>HTTP_Request_configuration</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base path</p></td><td><p>/</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>HTTP(Default)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><p>80</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use persistent connections</p></td><td><p>â</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection idle timeout</p></td><td><p>30000</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response buffer size</p></td><td><p>1024</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authentication</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client socket properties</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use reconnection</p></td><td><p>unchecked</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

30. Drag an **HTTP Request** onto the canvas and configure the following parameters:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p>Request - Box Upload File</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>Select the HTTP request configuration that you created in global elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Method</p></td><td><p>POST</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>URL</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://upload.box.com/api/2.0/files/content">https://upload.box.com/api/2.0/files/content</a></code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><p>payload</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

31. Create an Authorization header in the HTTP Request.
32. Click the **Headers** tab next to the Body in the HTTP request and click **+** to configure the
    following parameter:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>"Authorization"</p></td><td><p><code>vars.developerToken</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

33. Drag the DataWeave component to set the payload to display the result of the flow to the user
    and configure as follows:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	result : "File has been successfully created"
    }
    ```

34. Save and run the project as a Mule application.
35. Enter the URL `[http://localhost:8081/boxUploadFile](http://localhost:8081/boxUploadFile)` in
    the browser. You should get a JSON response with the following content:

    `result : "File has been successfully created"`

## XML Example: Upload File

Here is the XML example to upload a file.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:mule-box-connector="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector/current/mule-mule-box-connector.xsd">

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config">
		<file:connection workingDir="${mule.home}/apps/${app.name}/" />
	</file:config>

	<http:request-config name="HTTP_Request_configuration" doc:name="HTTP Request configuration" >
		<http:request-connection host="80" />
	</http:request-config>

	<flow name="UploadFileToBox" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/boxUploadFile" />
		<ee:transform doc:name="Set Developer Token Variable">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
		//Set the generated developer token here
var developerToken = "{developer Token}"
---
"Bearer " ++ developerToken]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
			<ee:variables>
			</ee:variables>
		</ee:transform>
		<set-variable value="#[payload]" doc:name="Save developer token" variableName="developerToken"/>
		<file:read doc:name="Read File" config-ref="File_Config" path="input/yourFileName"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform to multipart/form-data">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output multipart/form-data
---
{
  parts : {
  	attributes : {
  			headers : {
        		"Content-Type": "application/json"},
      		content : {
      			"name": message.attributes.fileName ,
	// Set the ID of the parent folder. Use "0" for the root folder.
      	 		"parent": {
      	 		  "id":"0"
				}
			}
	},
    file : {
      headers : {
        "Content-Disposition" : {
            "name": "file",
            "filename": message.attributes.fileName
        }
      },
      content : payload
    }
  }
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<http:request method="POST" doc:name="Request - Box Upload File" url="https://upload.box.com/api/2.0/files/content" config-ref="HTTP_Request_configuration">
			<http:headers ><![CDATA[#[output application/java
---
{
	"Authorization" : vars.developerToken,
	"Key" : "Value"
}]]]></http:headers>
		</http:request>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Response">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	result : "File has been successfully created"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Box Connector Reference 5.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] This topic is large and might take longer than usual to load in your browser. Allow extra
> time for the content to render or switch to another browser.

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

Anypoint Connector for Box (Box Connector) provides access to the Box Content API for secure content
management and content experience features for use in your own app. The connector is RESTful and is
organized around the main resources youâre familiar with from the Box web interface.

## Configurations

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_Connection">JWT</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2security">Oauth2security Connection Provider</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### JWT

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit which qualifies the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection will be closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections that will be kept open at the same time</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the connection <strong>Idle Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not received responses should be streamed</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response will be stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Public Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the Public Key configured in your Box instance to authenticate the application.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of the Entity ID: EnterpriseId or UserId</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Box Sub Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ENTERPRISE</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The box sub type: enterprise or user</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one key.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keystore</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to keystore</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keystore Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password of key store</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias which identifies the key</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key password</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Oauth2security Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of timeout for establishing connections to the remote service</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit which qualifies the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request is concurrently.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit which qualifies the connection <strong>Idle Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to stream received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are HTTP and HTTPS.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumerKey, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumerSecret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs authorization endpoint URL</p></td><td><p><code>https://account.box.com/api/oauth2/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL</p></td><td><p><code>https://api.box.com/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, the values default to those in the annotation.</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>root_readwrite</p></li><li><p>manage_app_users</p></li><li><p>manage_managed_users</p></li><li><p>manage_groups</p></li><li><p>manage_webhook</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource owner ID each component should use if the resource owner ID is not otherwise referenced.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a flow to execute immediately after an access token is received.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to an <code>&lt;http:listener-config /&gt;</code> to use to create the listener that will receive the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path of the access token callback endpoint</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path of the local http endpoint which triggers the OAuth dance</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the callback endpoint is behind a proxy or should be accessed through a non direct URL, use this parameter to tell the OAuth provider the URL it should use to access the callback</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

## Add domain to list of allowed collaboration domains

`<box:create-collaboration-whitelist-entries>`

Creates a new entry in the list of domains allowed to collaborate. This operation makes an HTTP POST
request to the /collaboration_whitelist_entries endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create collaboration

`<box:create-collaborations>`

Adds a collaboration for a single user or a single group to a file or folder. Collaborations can be
created using email address, user IDs, or a group IDs. If a collaboration is being created with a
group, access to this endpoint is dependent on the groupâs ability to be invited. This operation
makes an HTTP POST request to the /collaborations endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>notify</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines if users should receive email notification for the action performed.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create comment

`<box:create-comments>`

Adds a comment by the user to a specific file, or as a reply to an other comment. This operation
makes an HTTP POST request to the /comments endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Copy file request

`<box:create-file-requests-copy-by-file-request-id>`

Copies an existing file request that is present on one folder, and applies it to another folder.
This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /file_requests/{file_request_id}/copy endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File request id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file request. The ID for any file request can be determined by visiting a file request builder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/filerequest/123">https://*.app.box.com/filerequest/123</a></code> the <code>file_request_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for the request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Restore file

`<box:create-files-by-file-id>`

Restores a file that was moved to the trash. An optional new parent ID can be provided to restore
the file to in case the original folder has been deleted. This operation makes an HTTP POST request
to the /files/{file_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Upload file

`<box:create-files-content>`

Uploads a small file to Box. For file sizes over 50MB, use the Chunk Upload APIs.

In the Request body, the `attributes` part of the body must come **before** the `file` part.
Requests that do not follow this format when uploading the file will receive an HTTP `400` error
with a `metadata_after_file_contents` error code.

This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files/content endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content md5</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An optional header containing the SHA1 hash of the file to ensure that the file was not corrupted in transit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes - Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content of the 'attributes' part.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File - Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content of the 'file' part.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>file Filename</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The filename of the 'file' part (just the name, do not include path).</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Upload file version

`<box:create-files-content-by-file-id>`

Updates a fileâs content. For file sizes over 50MB, use the Chunk Upload APIs.

For the Request body order, the `attributes` part of the body must come **before** the `file` part.
Requests that do not follow this format when uploading the file will receive an HTTP `400` error
with a `metadata_after_file_contents` error code.

This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files/{file_id}/content endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Copy file

`<box:create-files-copy-by-file-id>`

Creates a copy of a file. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files/{file_id}/copy
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. The ID for any file can be determined by visiting a file in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Create metadata instance on file

`<box:create-files-metadata-by-file-id-scope-template-key>`

Applies an instance of a metadata template to a file. In most cases only values that are present in
the metadata template will be accepted, with the exception of the `global.properties` template,
which accepts any key-value pair.

This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files/{file_id}/metadata/{scope}/{template_key}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. The ID for any file can be determined by visiting a file in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GLOBAL</p></li><li><p>ENTERPRISE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Add classification to file

`<box:create-files-metadata-enterprise-security-classification6vmvochw-uwo-by-file-id>`

Adds a classification to a file by specifying the label of the classification to add. This API can
also be called by including the enterprise ID in the URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/{file_id}/metadata/enterprise/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo endpoint.

## Create upload session

`<box:create-files-upload-sessions>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
