## Delete user

`<box:delete-users-by-user-id>`

Deletes a user. By default, this operation will fail if the user still owns any content. Move the
userâs owned content before proceeding, or use the `force` field to delete the user and their files.
This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /users/{user_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>notify</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the user will receive email notification of the deletion</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>force</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the user should be deleted even if this user still own files</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Permanently remove web link

`<box:delete-web-links-trash-by-web-link-id>`

Permanently deletes a web link that is in the trash. This action cannot be undone. This operation
makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /web_links/{web_link_id}/trash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Web link id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the web link.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## List allowed collaboration domains

`<box:get-collaboration-whitelist-entries>`

Returns the list of domains that are deemed safe to create collaborations for within the current
enterprise. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /collaboration_whitelist_entries
endpoint.

### Parameters

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

GET request to the /collaborations endpoint.

## List all collections

`<box:get-collections>`

Retrieves a list of all collections for a given user. Currently, only the `favorites` collection is
supported. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /collections endpoint.

### Parameters

## List collection items

`<box:get-collections-items-by-collection-id>`

request to the /collections/{collection_id}/items endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Collection id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the collection.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, in addition to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Get comment

`<box:get-comments-by-comment-id>`

Retrieves the message and metadata for a specific comment, as well as information on the user who
created the comment. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /comments/{comment_id}
endpoint.

## List enterprise device pins

`<box:get-enterprises-device-pinners-by-enterprise-id>`

Retrieves a list of all the device pins within an enterprise. The user must have admin privileges,
and the application needs the `manage enterprise` scope to make this call. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /enterprises/{enterprise_id}/device_pinners endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enterprise id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the enterprise</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. ## List user and enterprise events

`<box:get-events>`

Returns up to a year of past events for a given user or for the entire enterprise. By default, this
returns events for the authenticated user. To retrieve events for the entire enterprise, set the
`stream_type` to `admin_logs`. The user making the API call must have admin privileges, and the
application must have the permission to access the event feed to get the enterprise event feed. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /events endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>CHANGES</p></li><li><p>SYNC</p></li><li><p>ADMIN_LOGS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the type of events that are returned * <code>all</code> returns everything for a user and is the default * <code>changes</code> returns events that may cause file tree changes such as file updates or collaborations. * <code>sync</code> is similar to <code>changes</code> but only applies to synced folders * <code>admin_logs</code> returns all events for an entire enterprise and requires the user making the API call to have admin permissions.</p></td><td><p>ALL</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream position</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location in the event stream to start receiving events from. * <code>now</code> will return an empty list events and the latest stream position for initialization. * <code>0</code> or <code>null</code> will return all events.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the number of events returned</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ACCESS_GRANTED</p></li><li><p>ACCESS_REVOKED</p></li><li><p>ADD_DEVICE_ASSOCIATION</p></li><li><p>ADD_LOGIN_ACTIVITY_DEVICE</p></li><li><p>ADMIN_LOGIN</p></li><li><p>APPLICATION_CREATED</p></li><li><p>APPLICATION_PUBLIC_KEY_ADDED</p></li><li><p>APPLICATION_PUBLIC_KEY_DELETED</p></li><li><p>CHANGE_ADMIN_ROLE</p></li><li><p>CHANGE_FOLDER_PERMISSION</p></li><li><p>COLLABORATION_ACCEPT</p></li><li><p>COLLABORATION_EXPIRATION</p></li><li><p>COLLABORATION_INVITE</p></li><li><p>COLLABORATION_REMOVE</p></li><li><p>COLLABORATION_ROLE_CHANGE</p></li><li><p>COMMENT_CREATE</p></li><li><p>COMMENT_DELETE</p></li><li><p>CONTENT_WORKFLOW_ABNORMAL_DOWNLOAD_ACTIVITY</p></li><li><p>CONTENT_WORKFLOW_AUTOMATION_ADD</p></li><li><p>CONTENT_WORKFLOW_AUTOMATION_DELETE</p></li><li><p>CONTENT_WORKFLOW_POLICY_ADD</p></li><li><p>CONTENT_WORKFLOW_SHARING_POLICY_VIOLATION</p></li><li><p>CONTENT_WORKFLOW_UPLOAD_POLICY_VIOLATION</p></li><li><p>COPY</p></li><li><p>DATA_RETENTION_CREATE_RETENTION</p></li><li><p>DATA_RETENTION_REMOVE_RETENTION</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li><li><p>DELETE_USER</p></li><li><p>DEVICE_TRUST_CHECK_FAILED</p></li><li><p>DOWNLOAD</p></li><li><p>EDIT</p></li><li><p>EDIT_USER</p></li><li><p>EMAIL_ALIAS_CONFIRM</p></li><li><p>EMAIL_ALIAS_REMOVE</p></li><li><p>ENTERPRISE_APP_AUTHORIZATION_UPDATE</p></li><li><p>EXTERNAL_COLLAB_SECURITY_SETTINGS</p></li><li><p>FAILED_LOGIN</p></li><li><p>FILE_MARKED_MALICIOUS</p></li><li><p>FILE_WATERMARKED_DOWNLOAD</p></li><li><p>GROUP_ADD_ITEM</p></li><li><p>GROUP_ADD_USER</p></li><li><p>GROUP_CREATION</p></li><li><p>GROUP_DELETION</p></li><li><p>GROUP_EDITED</p></li><li><p>GROUP_REMOVE_ITEM</p></li><li><p>GROUP_REMOVE_USER</p></li><li><p>ITEM_MODIFY</p></li><li><p>ITEM_OPEN</p></li><li><p>ITEM_SHARED_UPDATE</p></li><li><p>ITEM_SYNC</p></li><li><p>ITEM_UNSYNC</p></li><li><p>LEGAL_HOLD_ASSIGNMENT_CREATE</p></li><li><p>LEGAL_HOLD_ASSIGNMENT_DELETE</p></li><li><p>LEGAL_HOLD_POLICY_CREATE</p></li><li><p>LEGAL_HOLD_POLICY_ Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Get file request

`<box:get-file-requests-by-file-request-id>`

Retrieves the information about a file request. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/file_requests/{file_request_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File request id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file request. ## List file version legal holds

`<box:get-file-version-legal-holds>`

Gets a list of file versions on legal hold for a legal hold assignment. Due to ongoing
rearchitecture efforts, this API might not return all file versions for this policy ID. Instead,
this API will only return file versions held in the legacy architecture.

Two new endpoints are available to request any file versions held in the new architecture. For file
versions held in the new architecture, the
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/file_versions_on_hold` API can be used to return all past
file versions available for this policy assignment, and the
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/files_on_hold` API can be used to return any current
(latest) versions of a file under legal hold. The
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to find a list of policy
assignments for a given policy ID. After the rearchitecture is completed, this API will be
deprecated.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /file_version_legal_holds endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Policy id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the legal hold policy to get the file version legal holds for.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## List file version retentions

`<box:get-file-version-retentions>`

Retrieves all file version retentions for the given enterprise. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /file_version_retentions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by files with this ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File version id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by file versions with this ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Policy id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by the retention policy with this ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disposition action</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PERMANENTLY_DELETE</p></li><li><p>REMOVE_RETENTION</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by the retention policy with this disposition action.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disposition before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by files that will have their disposition come into effect before this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disposition after</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results by files that will have their disposition come into effect after this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Get file information

`<box:get-files-by-file-id>`

Retrieves the details about a file. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{file_id}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. The ID for any file can be determined by visiting a file in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested. Additionally this field can be used to query any metadata applied to the file by specifying the <code>metadata</code> field as well as the scope and key of the template to retrieve, for example <code>?field=metadata.enterprise_12345.contractTemplate</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If none match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ensures an item is returned only if it has changed. Pass in the itemâs last observed <code>etag</code> value into this header and the endpoint will fail with a <code>304 Not Modified</code> if the item has not changed since.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>boxapi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL, and optional password, for the shared link of this item. This header can be used to access items that have not been explicitly shared with a user. Use the format <code>shared_link=[link]</code> or if a password is required then use <code>shared_link=[link]&amp;shared_link_password=[password]</code>. This header can be used on the file or folder shared, as well as on any files or folders nested within the item.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X rep hints</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A header required to request specific <code>representations</code> of a file. Use this in combination with the <code>fields</code> query parameter to request a specific file representation. The general format for these representations is <code>X-Rep-Hints: [â¦â]</code> where <code>[â¦â]</code> is one or many hints in the format <code>[fileType?query]</code>. For example, to request a <code>png</code> representation in <code>32x32</code> as well as <code>94x94</code> pixel dimensions provide the following hints. <code>X-Rep-Hints: [jpg?dimensions=32x32][jpg?dimensions=94x94]</code> Additionally, a <code>text</code> representation is available for all document file types in Box using the <code>[extracted_text]</code> representation. <code>X-Rep-Hints: [extracted_text]</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Download file

`<box:get-files-content-by-file-id>`

Returns the contents of a file in binary format. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{file_id}/content endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. The ID for any file can be determined by visiting a file in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The file version to download</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An optional access token that can be used to pre-authenticate this request, which means that a download link can be shared with a browser or a third party service without them needing to know how to handle the authentication. When using this parameter, please make sure that the access token is sufficiently scoped down to only allow read access to that file and no other files or folders.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>range</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The byte range of the content to download. The format <code>{start_byte}-{end_byte}</code> can be used to specify what section of the file to download.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>boxapi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL, and optional password, for the shared link of this item. This header can be used to access items that have not been explicitly shared with a user. Use the format <code>shared_link=[link]</code> or if a password is required then use <code>shared_link=[link]&amp;shared_link_password=[password]</code>. This header can be used on the file or folder shared, as well as on any files or folders nested within the item.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Get classification on file

`<box:get-files-metadata-enterprise-security-classification6vmvochw-uwo-by-file-id>`

Retrieves the classification metadata instance that has been applied to a file. This API can also be
called by including the enterprise ID in the URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /files/{file_id}/metadata/enterprise/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo endpoint.

## List tasks on file

## Get file thumbnail

`<box:get-files-thumbnail-by-file-id-extension>`

Retrieves a thumbnail, or smaller image representation, of a file. Sizes of `32x32`,`64x64`,
`128x128`, and `256x256` can be returned in the `.png` format and sizes of `32x32`, `94x94`,
`160x160`, and `320x320` can be returned in the `.jpg` format.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{file_id}/thumbnail.{extension} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>extension</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PNG</p></li><li><p>JPG</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The file format for the thumbnail</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min height</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minimum height of the thumbnail</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min width</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minimum width of the thumbnail</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max height</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum height of the thumbnail</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max width</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum width of the thumbnail</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Get trashed file

`<box:get-files-trash-by-file-id>`

Retrieves a file that has been moved to the trash. The file can only be retrieved only if the file
itself has been moved to the trash. If instead one of its parent folders was moved to the trash,
only that folder can be inspected using the \[`GET /folders/:id/trash`\](e://get_folders_id_trash)
API. To list all items that have been moved to the trash, use the
\[`GET /folders/trash/items`\](e://get-folders-trash-items/) API.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{file_id}/trash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. The ID for any file can be determined by visiting a file in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## List all file versions

`<box:get-files-versions-by-file-id>`

Retrieves a list of the past versions for a file. Versions are only tracked by Box users with
premium accounts. To fetch the ID of the current version of a file, use the `GET /file/:id` API.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{file_id}/versions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. /folder_locks endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. The ID for any folder can be determined by visiting this folder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Get folder information

`<box:get-folders-by-folder-id>`

Retrieves details for a folder, including the first 100 entries in the folder. To fetch more items
within the folder, use the \[Get items in a folder\](#get-folders-id-items) endpoint. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /folders/{folder_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested. Additionally this field can be used to query any metadata applied to the file by specifying the <code>metadata</code> field as well as the scope and key of the template to retrieve, for example <code>?field=metadata.enterprise_12345.contractTemplate</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If none match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ensures an item is only returned if it has changed. ## List folder collaborations

`<box:get-folders-collaborations-by-folder-id>`

Retrieves a list of pending and active collaborations for a folder. This returns all the users that
have access to the folder or have been invited to the folder. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /folders/{folder_id}/collaborations endpoint.

In Box Connector 5.2.2 and later, this operation no longer uses pagination since Box changed its
underlying API endpoint, so the **offset** and **limit** fields are now ignored but are present only
for backward compatibility.

### Parameters

## Get shared link for folder

## List items in folder

`<box:get-folders-items-by-folder-id>`

Retrieves a page of items in a folder. These items can be files, folders, and web links. To request
more information about the folder itself, like its size, use the \[Get a folder\](#get-folders-id)
endpoint instead. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /folders/{folder_id}/items
endpoint. The operation automatically paginates the results using offset pagination.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. The ID for any folder can be determined by visiting this folder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested. Additionally this field can be used to query any metadata applied to the file by specifying the <code>metadata</code> field as well as the scope and key of the template to retrieve, for example <code>?field=metadata.enterprise_12345.contractTemplate</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>DO NOT USE - usemarker</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies whether to use marker-based pagination instead of offset-based pagination. Only one pagination method can be used at a time. By setting this value to true, the API will return a <code>marker</code> field that can be passed as a parameter to this endpoint to get the next page of the response.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>DO NOT USE - marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ID</p></li><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>DATE</p></li><li><p>SIZE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the <strong>second</strong> attribute by which items are sorted. Items are always sorted by their <code>type</code> first, with folders listed before files, and files listed before web links. This parameter is not supported for marker-based pagination on the root folder (the folder with an ID of <code>0</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>direction</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASC</p></li><li><p>DESC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The direction to sort results in. This can be either in alphabetical ascending (<code>ASC</code>) or descending (<code>DESC</code>) order.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>boxapi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL, and optional password, for the shared link of this item. This header can be used to access items that have not been explicitly shared with a user. Use the format <code>shared_link=[link]</code> or if a password is required then use <code>shared_link=[link]&amp;shared_link_password=[password]</code>. This header can be used on the file or folder shared, as well as on any files or folders nested within the item.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List metadata instances on folder

`<box:get-folders-metadata-by-folder-id>`

Retrieves all metadata for a given folder. This can not be used on the root folder with ID `0`. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /folders/{folder_id}/metadata endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Get metadata instance on folder

`<box:get-folders-metadata-by-folder-id-scope-template-key>`

Retrieves the instance of a metadata template that has been applied to a folder. This cannot be used
on the root folder with an ID of `0`. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/folders/{folder_id}/metadata/{scope}/{template_key} endpoint.

### Parameters

## Get classification on folder

`<box:get-folders-metadata-enterprise-security-classification6vmvochw-uwo-by-folder-id>`

Retrieves the classification metadata instance that has been applied to a folder. This API can also
be called by including the enterprise ID in the URL explicitly, for example
`/folders/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /folders/{folder_id}/metadata/enterprise/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo
endpoint.

## Get trashed folder

`<box:get-folders-trash-by-folder-id>`

Retrieves a folder that has been moved to the trash. If only the folder itself has been moved to the
trash, it can be retrieved with this API call. If instead, one of its parent folders was moved to
the trash, that folder can only be inspected using the
\[`GET /folders/:id/trash`\](e://get_folders_id_trash) API. To list all items that have been moved
to the trash, use the \[`GET /folders/trash/items`\](e://get-folders-trash-items/) API. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /folders/{folder_id}/trash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. The ID for any folder can be determined by visiting this folder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## List trashed items

`<box:get-folders-trash-items>`

Retrieves the files and folders that have been moved to the trash. Any attribute in the full files
or folders objects can be passed in with the `fields` parameter to retrieve those specific
attributes that are not returned by default. This endpoint defaults to use offset-based pagination,
and also supports marker-based pagination using the `marker` parameter. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /folders/trash/items endpoint. The operation automatically paginates the results
using offset pagination.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>DO NOT USE - usemarker</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies whether to use marker-based pagination instead of offset-based pagination. Only one pagination method can be used at a time. By setting this value to true, the API will return a <code>marker</code> field that can be passed as a parameter to this endpoint to get the next page of the response.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>DO NOT USE - marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>direction</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ASC</p></li><li><p>DESC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The direction to sort results in. This can be either in alphabetical ascending (<code>ASC</code>) or descending (<code>DESC</code>) order.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ID</p></li><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>DATE</p></li><li><p>SIZE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the <strong>second</strong> attribute by which items are sorted. Items are always sorted by their <code>type</code> first, with folders listed before files, and files listed before web links. This parameter is not supported when using marker-based pagination.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get watermark for folder

`<box:get-folders-watermark-by-folder-id>`

/folders/{folder_id}/watermark endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. ## Get group membership

`<box:get-group-memberships-by-group-membership-id>`

Retrieves a specific group membership. Only admins of this group or users with admin-level
permissions will be able to use this API. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/group_memberships/{group_membership_id} endpoint.

## List groups for enterprise

Retrieves all of the groups for a given enterprise. The user must have admin permissions to inspect
enterpriseâs groups. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /groups endpoint.

## Get group

`<box:get-groups-by-group-id>`

Retrieves information about a group. Only members of this group or users with admin-level
permissions will be able to use this API. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/groups/{group_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the group.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. /legal_hold_policies/{legal_hold_policy_id} endpoint

### Parameters

## List legal hold policy assignments

`<box:get-legal-hold-policy-assignments>`

Retrieves a list of items a legal hold policy has been assigned to. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /legal_hold_policy_assignments endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Policy id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the legal hold policy</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Assign to type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>FILE</p></li><li><p>FILE_VERSION</p></li><li><p>FOLDER</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Filters the results by the type of item the policy was applied to.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Assign to id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters the results by the ID of item the policy was applied to.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. ## List previous file versions for legal hold policy assignment

`<box:get-legal-hold-policy-assignments-file-versions-on-hold-by-legal-hold-policy-assignment-id>`

Get a list of previous file versions for a legal hold assignment. In some cases you may only need
the latest file versions instead. In these cases, use the
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/files_on_hold` API instead to return any current (latest)
versions of a file for this legal hold policy assignment. Due to ongoing re-architecture efforts
this API might not return all files held for this policy ID. Instead, this API will only return past
file versions held in the newly developed architecture. The `GET /file_version_legal_holds` API can
be used to fetch current and past versions of files held within the legacy architecture. The
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to find a list of policy
assignments for a given policy ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/legal_hold_policy_assignments/{legal_hold_policy_assignment_id}/file_versions_on_hold endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Legal hold policy assignment id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the legal hold policy assignment</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## List current file versions for legal hold policy assignment

`<box:get-legal-hold-policy-assignments-files-on-hold-by-legal-hold-policy-assignment-id>`

Gets a list of current file versions for a legal hold assignment. In some cases you may want to get
previous file versions instead. In these cases, use the
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/file_versions_on_hold` API instead to return any previous
versions of a file for this legal hold policy assignment.

Due to ongoing rearchitecture efforts, this API might not return all file versions held for this
policy ID. Instead, this API will return only the latest file version held in the newly developed
architecture. The `GET /file_version_legal_holds` API can be used to fetch current and past versions
of files held within the legacy architecture. The
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to find a list of policy
assignments for a given policy ID.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/legal_hold_policy_assignments/{legal_hold_policy_assignment_id}/files_on_hold endpoint.
