This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

## List metadata cascade policies

`<box:get-metadata-cascade-policies>`

Retrieves a list of all the metadata cascade policies that are applied to a given folder. This can
not be used on the root folder with ID `0`. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/metadata_cascade_policies endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which folder to return policies for. This can not be used on the root folder with ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Owner enterprise id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the enterprise ID for which to find metadata cascade policies. If not specified, it defaults to the current enterprise.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. ## Get metadata template by name

`<box:get-metadata-templates-schema-by-scope-template-key>`

Retrieves a metadata template by its `scope` and `templateKey` values. To find the `scope` and
`templateKey` for a template, list all templates for an enterprise or globally, or list all
templates applied to a file or folder. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/metadata_templates/{scope}/{template_key}/schema endpoint.

## Get events long poll endpoint

`<box:get-options-events>`

Returns a list of realtime servers that can be used for long-polling updates to the \[event
stream\](#get-events). In long polling, an HTTP request is kept open until the server sends a
response, and the process is repeated over and over to receive updated responses. Long polling the
event stream can only be used for user events, not for enterprise events.

To use long polling:

1.  First, use this endpoint to retrieve a list of long poll URLs.
2.  Next, make a long poll request to any of the provided URLs.

    When an event occurs in monitored account a response with the value `new_change` will be sent.
    The response contains no other details as it only serves as a prompt to take further action such
    as sending a request to the \[events endpoint\](#get-events) with the last known
    `stream_position`.

3.  After the server sends this response, it closes the connection.
4.  You must now repeat the long poll process to begin listening for events again.

    If no events occur for a while and the connection times out, you will receive a response with
    the value `reconnect`. When you receive this response, youâll make another call to this endpoint
    to restart the process. If you receive no events in `retry_timeout` seconds then you will need
    to make another request to the real-time server (one of the URLs in the response for this
    endpoint). This might be necessary due to network errors.

5.  Finally, if you receive a `max_retries` error when making a request to the realtime server, you
    should start over by making a call to this endpoint first.

This operation makes an HTTP OPTIONS request to the /events endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Preflight check before upload

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List recently accessed items

`<box:get-recent-items>`

Returns information about the recent items accessed by a user, either in the last 90 days or up to
the last 1000 items accessed. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /recent_items
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## List retention policies

`<box:get-retention-policies>`

to the /retention_policies endpoint

### Parameters

/retention_policies/{retention_policy_id}/assignments endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retention policy assignment id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the retention policy assignment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- BOX:BAD_REQUEST
- BOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- BOX:CONNECTIVITY
- BOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- BOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- BOX:NOT_FOUND
- BOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- BOX:SERVER_ERROR
- BOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- BOX:TIMEOUT
- BOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS

- BOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Search for content

`<box:get-search>`

Searches for files, folders, web links, and shared files across the users content or across the
entire enterprise. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /search endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>query</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The string to search for. This query is matched against item names, descriptions, text content of files, and various other fields of the different item types.</p></div><div><p>This parameter supports a variety of operators to further refine the results returns.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>""</code> Wrapping a query in double quotes means only exact matches are returned by the API. Exact searches do not return search matches based on specific character sequences. Instead, they return matches based on phrases, that is, word sequences, for example: A search for <code>"Blue-Box"</code> may return search results including the sequence <code>"blue.box"</code>, <code>"Blue Box"</code>, and <code>"Blue-Box"</code>; any item containing the words <code>Blue</code> and <code>Box</code> consecutively, in the order specified.</p></li><li><p><code>AND</code> returns items that contain both the search terms. For example, a search for <code>marketing AND BoxWorks</code> returns items that have both <code>marketing</code> and <code>BoxWorks</code> within its text in any order. It does not return a result that only has <code>BoxWorks</code> in its text.</p></li><li><p><code>OR</code> returns items that contain either of the search terms. For example, a search for <code>marketing OR BoxWorks</code> returns a result that has either <code>marketing</code> or <code>BoxWorks</code> within its text. Using this operator is not necessary as we implicitly interpret multi-word queries as <code>OR</code> unless another supported boolean term is used.</p></li><li><p><code>NOT</code> returns items that do not contain the search term provided. For example, a search for <code>marketing AND NOT BoxWorks</code> returns a result that has only <code>marketing</code> within its text. Results containing <code>BoxWorks</code> are omitted.</p></li></ul></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">Lower case (that is, <code>and</code>, <code>or</code>, and <code>not</code>) or mixed case (that is, <code>And</code>, <code>Or</code>, and <code>Not</code>) operators are not supported.</div></div><div><div><pre>This field is required unless the `mdfilters` parameter is defined.</pre></div></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>USER_CONTENT</p></li><li><p>ENTERPRISE_CONTENT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to either the files that the user has access to, or to files available to the entire enterprise. The scope defaults to <code>user_content</code>, which limits the search results to content that is available to the currently authenticated user. The <code>enterprise_content</code> can be requested by an admin through our support channels. Once this scope has been enabled for a user, it will allow that use to query for content across the entire enterprise and not only the content that they have access to.</p></td><td><p>USER_CONTENT</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File extensions</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to any files that match any of the provided file extensions. This list is a comma-separated list of file extensions without the dots.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created at range</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to any items created within a given date range. Date ranges are defined as comma-separated RFC3339 timestamps. If the the start date is omitted (<code>,2014-05-17T13:35:01-07:00</code>) anything created before the end date will be returned. If the end date is omitted (<code>2014-05-15T13:35:01-07:00,</code>) the current date will be used as the end date instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated at range</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to any items updated within a given date range. Date ranges are defined as comma-separated RFC3339 timestamps. If the start date is omitted (<code>,2014-05-17T13:35:01-07:00</code>) anything updated before the end date will be returned. If the end date is omitted (<code>2014-05-15T13:35:01-07:00,</code>) the current date will be used as the end date instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Size range</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to any items with a size within a given file size range. This applies to files and folders. Size ranges are defined as a comma-separated list of a lower and upper byte size limit (inclusive). The upper and lower bound can be omitted to create open ranges.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Owner user ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to any items that are owned by the given list of owners, defined as a list of comma separated user IDs. Please note that the items still need to be owned or shared with the currently authenticated user for them to show up in the search results. If the user does not have access to any files owned by any of the users an empty result set will be returned. To search across an entire enterprise, use the <code>enterprise_content</code> scope parameter, which can be requested from the Box support team.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ancestor folder ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to items within the given list of folders, defined as a comma-separated lists of folder IDs. Search results will also include items within any subfolders of those ancestor folders. Please note that the folders still need to be owned or shared with the currently authenticated user. If the folder is not accessible by this user, or it does not exist, a <code>HTTP 404</code> error code will be returned instead. To search across an entire enterprise, use the <code>enterprise_content</code> scope parameter, which can be requested from the Box support team.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content types</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>DESCRIPTION</p></li><li><p>FILE_CONTENT</p></li><li><p>COMMENTS</p></li><li><p>TAG</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the search results to any items that match the search query for a specific part of the file, for example the file description. Content types are defined as a comma-separated list of Box-recognized content types. The allowed content types are as follows:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>name</code> - The name of the item, as defined by its <code>name</code> field.</p></li><li><p><code>description</code> - The description of the item, as defined by its <code>description</code> field.</p></li><li><p><code>file_content</code> - The actual content of the file.</p></li><li><p><code>comments</code> - The content of any of the comments on a file or folder.</p></li><li><p><code>tag</code> - Any tags that are applied to an item, as defined by its <code>tag</code> field.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>FILE</p></li><li><p>FOLDER</p></li><li><p>WEB_LINK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the search results to any items of this type. This parameter accepts only one value. By default, the API returns items that match any of these types:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>file</code> - Limits the search results to files</p></li><li><p><code>folder</code> - Limits the search results to folders</p></li><li><p><code>web_link</code> - Limits the search results to web links, also known as bookmarks</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trash content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NON_TRASHED_ONLY</p></li><li><p>TRASHED_ONLY</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines if the search should look in the trash for items. By default, this API returns only search results for items not currently in the trash (<code>non_trashed_only</code>).</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>trashed_only</code> - Only searches for items currently in the trash</p></li><li><p><code>non_trashed_only</code> - Only searches for items currently not in the trash</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>NON_TRASHED_ONLY</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>mdfilters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the search results to any items for which the metadata matches the provided filter. This parameter contains a list of 1 metadata template to filter the search results by. This list can currently only contain one entry. This parameter is required unless the <code>query</code> parameter is provided.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MODIFIED_AT</p></li><li><p>RELEVANCE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the order in which search results are returned. This API defaults to returning items by relevance unless this parameter is explicitly specified:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>relevance</code> (default) returns the results sorted by relevance to the query search term. The relevance is based on the occurrence of the search term in the items name, description, content, and additional properties.</p></li><li><p><code>modified_at</code> returns the results ordered in descending order by date at which the item was last modified.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>RELEVANCE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>direction</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DESC</p></li><li><p>ASC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the direction in which search results are ordered. This API defaults to returning items in descending (<code>DESC</code>) order unless this parameter is explicitly specified. When results are sorted by <code>relevance</code> the ordering is locked to returning items in descending order of relevance, and this parameter is ignored.</p></td><td><p>DESC</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum number of items to return as part of a page of results.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include recent shared links</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether the search results should include any items that the user recently accessed through a shared link. Note that when this parameter is set to true, the format of the response of this API changes to return a list of [Search Results with Shared Links](r://search_results_with_shared_links)</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of * Any * Any * Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Find file for shared link

`<box:get-shared-items>`

Return the file represented by a shared link. A shared file can be represented by a shared link,
which can originate within the current enterprise or within another. This endpoint allows an
application to retrieve information about a shared file when only given a shared link. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /shared_items endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If none match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ensures an item is only returned if it has changed. Pass in the itemâs last observed <code>etag</code> value into this header and the endpoint will fail with a <code>304 Not Modified</code> if the item has not changed since.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>boxapi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A header containing the shared link and optional password for the shared link. The format for this header is as follows. <code>shared_link=[link]&amp;shared_link_password=[password]</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Find folder for shared link

`<box:get-shared-itemsfolders>`

Return the folder represented by a shared link. A shared folder can be represented by a shared link,
which can originate within the current enterprise or within another. This endpoint allows an
application to retrieve information about a shared folder when only given a shared link. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /shared_items#folders endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If none match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ensures an item is only returned if it has changed. Pass in the itemâs last observed <code>etag</code> value into this header and the endpoint will fail with a <code>304 Not Modified</code> if the item has not changed since.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>boxapi</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A header containing the shared link and optional password for the shared link. The format for this header is as follows. <code>shared_link=[link]&amp;shared_link_password=[password]</code></p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

/task_assignments/{task_assignment_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Task assignment id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the task assignment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List enterprise users

`<box:get-users>`

Returns a list of all users for the enterprise, along with their `user_id`, `public_name`, and
`login`. The application and the authenticated user must have the permission to look up users in the
entire enterprise. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users endpoint. The operation
automatically paginates the results using offset pagination.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter term</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the results to only users whoâs <code>name</code> or <code>login</code> start with the search term. For externally managed users, the search term needs to completely match the in order to find the user, and it will only return one user at a time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>MANAGED</p></li><li><p>EXTERNAL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the results to the kind of user specified.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>all</code> returns every kind of user for whom the <code>login</code> or <code>name</code> partially matches the <code>filter_term</code>. It only returns an external user if the login matches the <code>filter_term</code> completely, and in that case it only returns that user.</p></li><li><p><code>managed</code> returns all managed and app users for whom the <code>login</code> or <code>name</code> partially matches the <code>filter_term</code>.</p></li><li><p><code>external</code> returns all external users for whom the <code>login</code> matches the <code>filter_term</code> exactly.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>External app user id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Limits the results to app users with the given <code>external_app_user_id</code> value. When creating an app user, an <code>external_app_user_id</code> value can be set. This value can then be used in this endpoint to find any users that match that <code>external_app_user_id</code> value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The offset of the item at which to begin the response.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>DO NOT USE - usemarker</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies whether to use marker-based pagination instead of offset-based pagination. Only one pagination method can be used at a time. By setting this value to true, the API will return a <code>marker</code> field that can be passed as a parameter to this endpoint to get the next page of the response.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>DO NOT USE - marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Get user

`<box:get-users-by-user-id>`

Retrieves information about a user in the enterprise. The application and the authenticated user
must have the permission to look up users in the entire enterprise. This endpoint also returns a
limited set of information for external users who are collaborated on content owned by the
enterprise for authenticated users with the right scopes. In this case, disallowed fields will
return null instead. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/{user_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. - BOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- BOX:NOT_FOUND

## Get current user

`<box:get-users-me>`

Retrieves information about the user who is currently authenticated. In the case of a client-side
authenticated OAuth 2.0 application, this is the user who authorized the app. In the case of a JWT,
server-side authenticated application, this is the service account that belongs to the application
by default. Use the `As-User` header to change who this API call is made on behalf of. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /users/me endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## List userâs groups

## List all webhooks

`<box:get-webhooks>`

Returns all defined webhooks for the requesting application. This API returns only webhooks that are
applied to files or folders that are owned by the authenticated user. This means that an admin
cannot see webhooks created by a service account unless the admin has access to those folders, and
vice versa. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /webhooks endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>marker</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the position marker at which to begin returning results. This is used when paginating using marker-based pagination. This requires <code>usemarker</code> to be set to <code>true</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of items to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webhook id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the webhook.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Download zip archive

`<box:get-zip-downloads-content-by-zip-download-id>`

Returns the contents of a ZIP archive in binary format. This URL does not require any form of
authentication and could be used in a userâs browser to download the archive to a userâs device. By
default, this URL is only valid for a few seconds from the creation of the request for this archive.

After a download has started it cannot be stopped and resumed, instead a new request for a ZIP
archive must be created. The URL of this endpoint should not be considered as fixed. Instead, use
the \[Create zip download\](e://post_zip_downloads) API to request the creation of a ZIP archive,
and then follow the `download_url` field in the response to this endpoint.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /zip_downloads/{zip_download_id}/content endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>(DEPRECATED) Zip download id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents this <code>zip</code> archive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zip download URL generated by 'Create zip download' operation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The temporary URL to download the <code>zip</code> archive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- BOX:CLIENT_ERROR

## Get zip download status

`<box:get-zip-downloads-status-by-zip-download-id>`

Returns the download status of a ZIP archive, allowing an application to inspect the progress of the
download as well as the number of items that might have been skipped. This endpoint can only be
accessed after the download has started. Subsequently, this endpoint is valid for 12 hours from the
start of the download. The URL of this endpoint should not be considered as fixed. Instead, use the
\[Create zip download\](e://post_zip_downloads) API to request the creation of a ZIP archive, and
then follow the `status_url` field in the response to this endpoint.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /zip_downloads/{zip_download_id}/status endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zip download id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent this <code>zip</code> archive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Unauthorize

`<box:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that itâs impossible to
execute any operation for that user without repeating the authorization dance.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The id of the resource owner which access should be invalidated</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update file request

`<box:update-file-requests-by-file-request-id>`

Updates a file request. This can be used to activate or deactivate a file request. This operation
makes an HTTP PUT request to the /file_requests/{file_request_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File request id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file request. The ID for any file request can be determined by visiting a file request builder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/filerequest/123">https://*.app.box.com/filerequest/123</a></code> the <code>file_request_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>If match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ensures this item hasnât recently changed before making changes. Pass in the itemâs last observed <code>etag</code> value into this header and the endpoint will fail with a <code>412 Precondition Failed</code> if it has changed since.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update file

## Update metadata instance on file

`<box:update-files-metadata-by-file-id-scope-template-key>`

Updates a piece of metadata on a file. The metadata instance can only be updated if the template has
already been applied to the file before. When editing metadata, only values that match the metadata
template schema will be accepted. The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur during the
application of the operations, the metadata instance will not be changed. This operation makes an
HTTP PUT request to the /files/{file_id}/metadata/{scope}/{template_key} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represents a file. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GLOBAL</p></li><li><p>ENTERPRISE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
