## Upload part of file

`<box:update-files-upload-sessions-by-upload-session-id>`

Updates a chunk of an upload session for a file. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/files/upload_sessions/{upload_session_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upload session id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the upload session.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>digest</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The [RFC3230][1] message digest of the chunk uploaded. Only SHA1 is supported. The SHA1 digest must be Base64 encoded. The format of this header is as <code>sha=BASE64_ENCODED_DIGEST</code>. [1]: <a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3230">https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3230</a></p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content range</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The byte range of the chunk. Must not overlap with the range of a part already uploaded this session.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. For example, for the URL <code>https://*.app.box.com/files/123</code> the <code>file_id</code> is <code>123</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update metadata instance on folder

`<box:update-folders-metadata-by-folder-id-scope-template-key>`

Updates a piece of metadata on a folder. The metadata instance can only be updated if the template
has already been applied to the folder before. When editing metadata, only values that match the
metadata template schema will be accepted. The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur
during the application of the operations, the metadata instance will not be changed. This operation
makes an HTTP PUT request to the /folders/{folder_id}/metadata/{scope}/{template_key} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. The ID for any folder can be determined by visiting this folder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GLOBAL</p></li><li><p>ENTERPRISE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Remove shared link from folder

`<box:update-folders-remove-shared-link-by-folder-id>`

Removes a shared link from a folder. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/folders/{folder_id}#remove_shared_link endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Folder id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique identifier that represent a folder. The ID for any folder can be determined by visiting this folder in the web application and copying the ID from the URL. For example, for the URL <code><a href="https://*.app.box.com/folder/123">https://*.app.box.com/folder/123</a></code> the <code>folder_id</code> is <code>123</code>. The root folder of a Box account is always represented by the ID <code>0</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Explicitly request the <code>shared_link</code> fields to be returned for this item.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update metadata template

`<box:update-metadata-templates-schema-by-scope-template-key>`

Updates a metadata template. The metadata template can only be updated if the template already
exists. The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur during the application of the
operations, the metadata template will not be changed. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to
the /metadata_templates/{scope}/{template_key}/schema endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>scope</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GLOBAL</p></li><li><p>ENTERPRISE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The scope of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Template key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the metadata template</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. /retention_policies/{retention_policy_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retention policy id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the retention policy.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. ## Update user

`<box:update-users-by-user-id>`

Updates a managed user in an enterprise. This endpoint is only available to users and applications
with the right admin permissions. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /users/{user_id}
endpoint.

## Transfer owned folders

`<box:update-users-folders0-by-user-id>`

Moves all of the items (files, folders and workflows) owned by a user into another userâs account.
Only the root folder (`0`) can be transferred. Folders can only be moved across users by users with
administrative permissions.

This call is performed synchronously, which might lead to a slow response when the source user has a
large number of items in all of their folders. If the destination path has a metadata cascade policy
attached to any of the parent folders, a metadata cascade operation is kicked off asynchronously.
There is currently no way to check for when this operation is finished.

The destination folderâs name will be in the format `{User}'s Files and Folders`, where `{User}` is
the display name of the user.

To make this API call, your application must have the
`Read and write all files and folders stored in Box` scope enabled. Ensure the destination user has
access to `Relay` or `Relay Lite`, and has access to the files and folders involved in the workflows
being transferred. Admins will receive an email when the operation is completed.

This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /users/{user_id}/folders/0 endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of attributes to include in the response. This can be used to request fields that are not normally returned in a standard response. Be aware that specifying this parameter will have the effect that none of the standard fields are returned in the response unless explicitly specified, instead only fields for the mini representation are returned, additional to the fields requested.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>notify</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines if users should receive email notification for the action performed.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Updates a webhook. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /webhooks/{webhook_id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Webhook id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the webhook.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>X</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO automatically defines the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- BOX:CLIENT_ERROR
- BOX:CONNECTIVITY
- BOX:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- BOX:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- BOX:NOT_FOUND
- BOX:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- BOX:SERVER_ERROR
- BOX:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- BOX:TIMEOUT
- BOX:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- BOX:UNAUTHORIZED
- BOX:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Http Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Status Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason Phrase</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of memory that will be allocated in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded by according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much will be buffer size by expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower will mean that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of times that a delivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Identifier</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the non-repeatable streaming strategy by which Mule disables repeatable streams. Use this strategy if your use case requires reading the content only once.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

Configures how to identify a redelivered message and how to find out when the message was
redelivered.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Use Secure Hash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Expression</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is false.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the number of instances initially allowed to be kept in memory in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much the buffer size will be expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, and that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Objects</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of instances that will be kept in memory. If more than that is required, then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Configure Box Connector 5.3 Using Studio

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

You can use this connector in Anypoint Studio by first downloading it from Exchange and configuring
it for your application.

Configure a connector in Anypoint Studio:

1.  Add the connector to your project.
2.  Configure the connector.
3.  Configure an input source for the connector.

## Add the Connector to Your Studio Project

Anypoint Studio provides two ways to add the connector to your Studio project: from the Exchange
button in the Studio taskbar or from the Mule Palette view.

### Add the Connector Using Exchange

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  Click the Exchange icon **(X)** in the upper-left of the Studio task bar.
3.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
4.  In Exchange, search for "box".
5.  Select the connector and click **Add to project**.
6.  Follow the prompts to install the connector.

### Add the Connector in Studio

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type "box" in the search field.
4.  Click this connectorâs name in **Available modules**.
5.  Click **Add**.
6.  Click **Finish**.

## Configure the Connector

To use Box Connector in a Mule application, configure a global configuration element that can be
used by as many Box Connectors as you require for your application.

Enter the following information in the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure JWT authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Client id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The client identifier, as assigned by the authorization server when the client application was registered.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Client secret</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The client applicationâs client secret.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Public key id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the public key configured in your Box instance to authenticate the application.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity id</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of the entity ID: <code>EnterpriseId</code> or <code>UserId</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Box sub type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The box subtype: <code>enterprise</code> or <code>user</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Algorithm</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The RSA encryption algorithm used to encrypt the private key.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Keystore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Keystore type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The keystore type: <code>JKS</code> or <code>PKCS12</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Store password</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password of the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Key alias</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The alias that identifies the key.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Key password</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The key password.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Enter the following information in the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Oauth2security authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Consumer key</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Consumer secret</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Listener config</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Callback path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Authorize path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure an Input Source

Configure an input source for the connector such as a an operation for the Box connector, **HTTP
Listener**, or **Scheduler**. You can put the following operations for the connector in the
**Source** area of the Studio canvas.

**Configuration Name:** The configuration name for the Box connector global element.

**Host:** The host where Box exposes its API.

**Port:** The port where Box services listen for incoming requests.

**Base Path:** The base path of the Box serviceâs base URI.

**Protocol:** The protocol that Box services follow.

**Client Id:** The client identifier assigned by the authorization server when the client
application was registered.

**Client Secret:** The client applicationâs client secret.

**Local Callback Path:** The local callback path.

**Local Callback Config:** The HTTP Listener configuration that is used to configure the local
authorization URL.

**External Callback Url:** The redirect URL that is configured by the client application.

**Local Authorization Url:** The local authorization URL that is used to initiate an `OAuth2` dance.

**Authorization Url:** The authorization URL used to request an authorization code.

**Access Token Url:** The access token URL to request for an access token.

## Next Step

After configuring the connector for use in Studio, see the
[Additional Configuration Information](box-connector-config-topics) topic for more configuration
steps.

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Box Connector to Version 5.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Box (Box Connector) from version 4.x to version 5.x to take advantage
of new functionality.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>4.x.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5.x.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

- The prefix for all the connector operations changed from **mule-box-connector** to **box**.
- The OAuth 2.0 configuration XML structure changed.

  In earlier releases, the XML for the OAuth 2.0 connection element looked like this:

  ```xml
  <mule-box-connector:config
  	name="Mule_box_connector_Config"
  	doc:name="Mule-box-connector Config"
  	property_clientId="clientId"
  	property_clientSecret="secret"
  	property_localCallbackConfig="HTTP_Listener_config"
  	property_host="api.box.com"
  	property_port="4439"
  	property_basePath="/2.0"
  	property_protocol="HTTPS"
  	property_responseTimeout="10000"
  	property_localCallbackPath="/callback"
  	property_externalCallbackUrl="http://my-app.cloudhub.io/callback"
  	property_localAuthorizationUrl="http://localhost:8081/authorize"
  	property_authorizationUrl="https://account.box.com/api/oauth2/authorize"
  	property_accessTokenUrl="https://api.box.com/oauth2/token"
  />
  ```

  In the latest release, the XML looks like this:

  ```xml
  <box:config name="Box_Connector_Config" doc:name="Box Connector Config" >
  	<box:oauth2security-connection >
  		<box:oauth-authorization-code
  			consumerKey="consumerKey"
  			consumerSecret="consumerSecret"
  			authorizationUrl="https://account.box.com/api/oauth2/authorize"
  			accessTokenUrl="https://api.box.com/oauth2/token"
  			resourceOwnerId=âresourceownerIdâ />
  		<box:oauth-callback-config
  			listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config"
  			callbackPath="/callback"
  			authorizePath="/authorize"
  			externalCallbackUrl="http://localhost:8081/callback" />
  	</box:oauth2security-connection>
  </box:config>
  ```

## New Operations

New operations for Box Connector 5.0 include:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Box Operation</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Upload file</p></td><td><p>Uploads a file to Box. For files larger than 50 MB, use the <strong>Create upload session</strong> operation instead.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get file information</p></td><td><p>Retrieves detailed information about a file.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upload file version</p></td><td><p>POST operation that updates a fileâs contents. For files larger than 50 MB, use the <strong>Create upload session</strong> operation instead.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create upload session</p></td><td><p>Creates an upload session for a new file. Use this operation for uploading files larger than 50 MB. Chunks large uploads into sequential parts that you can then upload individually. Parts can be uploaded in parallel.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get upload session</p></td><td><p>Returns information about an upload session.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>List parts</p></td><td><p>Returns a list of the chunks uploaded to the upload session so far.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create upload session for existing file</p></td><td><p>Creates an upload session for an existing file.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Commit upload session</p></td><td><p>Closes an upload session and creates a file from the uploaded chunks.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upload part of file</p></td><td><p>Updates a chunk of an upload session for a file.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Remove upload session</p></td><td><p>Aborts an upload session and discards all data uploaded.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, you must create a backup of your files, data, and configurations in
case you need to restore to them.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `box` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, select **Box Connector Config** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the Box Connector dependency version is 5.0.0 in the `pom.xml` file in the Mule
    project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

## Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Verify that there are no problems in the project `pom.xml` file.

### Revert the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of box connector, change the `5.0.0` dependency
version in the projectâs `pom.xml` file to the previous version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` file in Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Box Connector Reference](box-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Box 5.3 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule application in XML either from the Studio XML editor or a text editor,
you must perform the following actions to get access to a connector:

1.  Add the namespace for the connector to your XML flow.
2.  Add dependency information to your Maven `pom.xml` file.

In the Studio visual editor, when you drag a connector from the palette to the canvas, Studio
automatically populates the XML code with the connector namespace and schema location.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag in the header of your XML configuration:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/box-connector/current/mule-mule-box-connector.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the mule XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:mule-box-connector="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-box-connector
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/box-connector/current/mule-mule-box-connector.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The pom.xml Maven file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app. When you
code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your pom.xml file to enable access to the
connector.

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-box-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information, access the connector in
[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) and click **Dependency Snippets**.

## Next

Now that you have read about XML support, you can try out the Box Examples
[Examples](box-connector-examples).

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Box Connector 5.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Box is an online file sharing and content management service for businesses. Anypoint Connector for
Box (Box Connector) is a RAML-based connector that provides synchronization of data and automation
of processes between Box and third party CRMs, CMS, mobile, and social applications. Because this is
a RAML-based connector, it only uses REST calls to interact with the Box 2.0 API and supports only a
subset of available API services.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an app, you must have access to the Box target resource and Anypoint Platform. You
must also understand how to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio.

You should be familiar with the use of the Box 2.0 API, Mule, Anypoint Connectors, Anypoint Studio,
Mule concepts, elements in a Mule flow, and global elements.

To use this connector you need the following:

- A developer account or other valid Box instance.
- MuleSoft Enterprise License and necessary entitlements.

## Connection Types

Box Connector uses the following authentication types:

- JWT  
  Server-side authentication using a keypair. For more information about configuring Box Connector
  with JWT, refer to [JWT Authentication](box-connector-config-topics#jwt).
- Oauth2security  
  Access tokens provided by the authorization server.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app with
[Anypoint Studio](box-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Box Connector on Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-box-connector/)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
